title: Die Wacht am Rhein : $b Roman
summaries: "Die Wacht am Rhein: Roman" by Clara Viebig is a historical novel published in 1902. Set in nineteenth-century Düsseldorf, the story traces three generations of the Rinke family as Rhinelanders gradually unite with their Prussian rulers. The tale begins with a controversial marriage between Protestant Prussian soldier Friedrich Rinke and Catholic Rhineland tavern-keeper's daughter Katharina, whose union faces family resistance and cultural conflict. As their children grow amid rising revolutionary fervor and social change, loyalties are tested and family bonds strained between military duty and personal conscience.
